data.title: Is Bayer (XTRA:BAYN) Still Attractive After A 79% One Year Share Price Rebound?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Bayer's current share price reflects its true value, the recent numbers give you quite a bit to work with.
  • At a last close of €40.04, the stock has returned 6.1% over the past month and 79.0% over the last year, while the 3 year and 5 year returns of 29.5% and 18.2% show a very different experience for longer term holders.
  • Recent headlines around Bayer have focused on its ongoing portfolio of pharmaceuticals and crop science products, as well as legal and restructuring developments that continue to shape investor sentiment. These themes help explain why shorter term returns look different to the longer term track record and why opinions on the stock are still divided.
  • Bayer currently holds a valuation score of 5 out of 6. This means it screens as undervalued on most of the checks used here. The next sections will break that down across several valuation methods before finishing with a different way to think about what "fair value" really means.

Bayer delivered 79.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Pharmaceuticals industry.

Approach 1: Bayer Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today to reflect risk and the time value of money.

For Bayer, the latest twelve month free cash flow is about €3.32b. Analysts and model estimates suggest annual free cash flow projections that reach €6.41b by 2030, with a detailed path of cash flows between 2026 and 2035 that includes both analyst inputs for the nearer years and extrapolated figures for the later years. All of these projected cash flows are converted into today’s terms using the 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model.

On this basis, the estimated intrinsic value in the model is €214.79 per share. Compared with the recent share price of €40.04, the model output implies an intrinsic discount of roughly 81.4%, indicating that the shares screen as significantly undervalued using this method alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Bayer is undervalued by 81.4%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 231 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Bayer.

Approach 2: Bayer Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings can be affected by one off items, the P/S ratio is often a useful cross check because it compares the share price to revenue, which is usually more stable than profit in any single year.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/S ratio will depend on how quickly revenue is expected to grow and how risky the business is. Higher growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/S multiple, while slower growth or higher risk can justify a lower one.

Bayer currently trades on a P/S ratio of 0.86x. This sits below the Pharmaceuticals industry average of 2.65x and also below the peer average of 2.45x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Bayer is 2.31x. This is a proprietary estimate of the P/S multiple that might be appropriate given factors such as Bayer’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market value and risk characteristics.

The Fair Ratio provides a more tailored benchmark than a simple comparison with peers or industry averages because it adjusts for company specific factors rather than assuming all firms should trade on the same multiple. With Bayer’s current P/S of 0.86x versus a Fair Ratio of 2.31x, the shares appear undervalued on this measure.

For Bayer, however, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Bayer narratives:

🐂 Bayer Bull Case

Implied discount to this fair value at the last close of €40.04: about 33% undervalued

Revenue growth assumption: 3.68% a year

  • Assumes Nubeqa, Kerendia and other newer products, along with sustainable agriculture offerings and digital farming, support higher revenue and margin levels than consensus expects.

data.title: Simona (DB:SIM0) Margin Squeeze And EPS Slide Challenge Bullish Earnings Recovery Narratives
data.description:
SIMONA (DB:SIM0) has reported its FY 2025 first half with revenue of €305.8 million and basic EPS of €1.08, setting the tone against a trailing twelve month backdrop of €590.0 million in revenue and EPS of €3.18. Over recent periods, the company has seen revenue move from €301.3 million in 1H 2024 to €283.7 million in 2H 2024 and then to €305.8 million in 1H 2025. Basic EPS shifted from €2.21 to €1.62 and then to €1.08. This pattern keeps the focus squarely on how profit margins are holding up through this latest reporting stretch.

See our full analysis for SIMONA.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the prevailing growth and risk narratives that investors have been watching over the past year.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

3.2% net margin puts profitability in context

  • Over the last 12 months, SIMONA produced €590.0 million in revenue and €19.1 million in net income, which works out to a 3.2% net margin compared with 3.9% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for a more cautious, bearish view is that this thinner 3.2% margin sits alongside five year earnings that declined by about 10.6% per year, which can make the recent drop from €13.2 million net income in 1H 2024 to €6.5 million in 1H 2025 look like part of a longer profit squeeze rather than a one off pause.
    • Bears point to that sequence of half year net income figures, €13.2 million, then €9.7 million, then €6.5 million, as evidence that profitability has been under pressure across multiple periods, not just this latest half.
    • The move in basic EPS over the same halves, from €2.21 to €1.62 to €1.08, lines up with that concern and gives the bearish side firm numbers to reference when they question how quickly margins can rebuild.

P/E of 16.8x versus peers and DCF fair value

  • The shares trade on a P/E of 16.8x at a price of €53.50, below the wider European Chemicals industry at 19.1x but above the peer group average of 10.3x, while a DCF fair value of €107.28 sits well above the current share price.
  • Supporters with a more bullish tilt argue that this mix, a P/E lower than the broader industry and a DCF fair value nearly twice the current €53.50 level, suggests the market is not fully pricing in the forecast 27% annual earnings growth, even though the premium to the 10.3x peer average keeps the debate open.
    • The projected 27% yearly earnings growth compared with the German market’s 16.0% gives bulls a clear growth angle to set against the current valuation multiples.
    • At the same time, the modest 4.3% expected annual revenue growth and the lower 3.2% trailing margin both pull in the other direction, so the bullish case rests heavily on profit per euro of sales improving from here rather than just on sales volume.

Revenue trend steady while growth forecasts diverge

  • On the top line, revenue across the last three halves has moved from €301.3 million in 1H 2024 to €283.7 million in 2H 2024 and then €305.8 million in 1H 2025, while forward looking estimates point to about 4.3% yearly revenue growth but around 27% yearly earnings growth.
  • What is interesting for readers comparing views is how this combination of relatively steady revenue and much stronger earnings forecasts contrasts with the recent five year earnings decline of about 10.6% per year, putting the focus on whether the business can convert similar revenue levels into higher profit without a big sales jump.
    • The trailing twelve month revenue line, sitting close together at €581.9 million, €586.4 million and €590.0 million over the last three data points, highlights that forecasts of faster earnings growth are not currently backed by a rapid sales ramp.
    • Alongside that, the forecast 4.3% revenue growth running slower than the 6.1% expected for the German market explains why some investors pay close attention to cost control and margin discipline rather than assuming that higher sales alone will carry future profit.

data.title: Is Prudential Financial (PRU) Pricing Look Misaligned After Recent Share Pullback?
data.description:

  • The stock has seen mixed returns, with a 5.4% decline over the past week, 0.5% decline over the last month, 17.1% decline year to date and a 4.5% decline over the past year, set against longer term gains of 28.1% over three years and 18.6% over five years.

That context helps frame whether recent moves reflect changing risk perception or a potential reset in expectations.

Approach 1: Prudential Financial Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to earn above the return that investors typically require on its equity, then capitalizes those "extra" profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For Prudential Financial, the starting point is its book value of $93.23 per share and a stable book value estimate of $107.86 per share, based on future book value estimates from 8 analysts. On this equity base, the company is expected to generate stable earnings of $14.57 per share, sourced from weighted future return on equity estimates from 12 analysts. That implies an average return on equity of 13.51%.

The required return, or cost of equity, is $8.74 per share, which leaves an excess return of $5.83 per share. When these expected excess returns are projected forward and discounted, the model arrives at an intrinsic value of about $232.26 per share.

Compared with the current share price of around $94.35, the Excess Returns model suggests Prudential Financial is trading at about a 59.4% discount, which indicates that the shares appear materially undervalued on this basis.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Prudential Financial Price vs Earnings

For a profitable insurer like Prudential Financial, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it tells you how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It is also widely used across the Insurance industry, which makes comparisons easier for you as an investor.

What counts as a "fair" P/E usually reflects the market's view on two things: how quickly earnings might grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk often lines up with a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tends to be associated with a lower multiple.

Prudential Financial currently trades on a P/E of 9.28x, compared with the Insurance industry average of 11.48x and a peer group average of 16.66x. Because it is tailored to Prudential Financial's fundamentals, it can provide a more focused reference point than a simple industry or peer comparison. QCR Holdings (QCRH) just wrapped up Q1 2026 with recent quarterly revenue running around US$101.5 million and basic EPS of about US$2.13, backed by trailing twelve month revenue of roughly US$351.5 million and EPS of US$7.54 that has been associated with an 11.7% earnings growth rate over the past year. Over recent periods, the company has seen revenue move from US$330.2 million and EPS of US$6.77 on a trailing basis in Q4 2024 to US$351.5 million and EPS of US$7.54 by Q4 2025, supported by a net profit margin of 36.2% compared with 34.5% the prior year. For investors, that kind of margin profile sets the stage for a results season in which the key question is how durable this profitability really looks.

See our full analysis for QCR Holdings.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up against the main narratives around QCR Holdings, and where the data starts to challenge those stories.

See what the community is saying about QCR Holdings

Advertisement

Margins and costs tracked through the year

  • Across the last twelve months, QCR Holdings reported a net profit margin of 36.2%, alongside a net interest margin of 3.49% and a cost to income ratio of 57.63%, giving a fuller picture of how much of that US$351.5 million in revenue is turning into profit after funding costs and operating expenses.
  • Analysts' consensus view links future earnings strength to digital transformation and wealth management growth, and the current margins create both support and questions for that view.
    • On one hand, the move from a 3.33% net interest margin and 58.37% cost to income ratio in the earlier trailing data to 3.49% and 57.63% aligns with the idea that efficiency projects and a better deposit mix can support profitability.
    • On the other hand, consensus expects profit margins to move from 36.2% to 27.8% over the next few years, which sits in tension with the recent margin profile and means the bullish efficiency story is not fully reflected in the forecasts.

Loan book steady with contained problem credits

  • Total loans at the end of 2025 sat at US$7.2b on a trailing twelve month basis, with non performing loans at US$42.3 million, which is a small share of the overall portfolio and gives context for the 11.7% earnings growth over the past year.
  • Critics in the consensus narrative focus on exposures such as commercial real estate, agriculture and the M2 equipment finance portfolio, and the current numbers give a mixed read on those concerns.
    • The presence of US$42.3 million in non performing loans plus references to runoff and charge offs in specific portfolios show that credit issues exist but are not dominating the overall loan book at this size.
    • At the same time, the mention of criticized loans ticking up from a single large downgrade and heavy use of areas like LIHTC and commercial real estate means bears can still point to areas where that US$7.2b of loans could be more sensitive if conditions in those segments change.
🐻 QCR Holdings Bear Case

Valuation gap versus earnings profile

  • QCR Holdings trades on a trailing P/E of 11.5x at a share price of US$88.56, compared with a DCF fair value of US$134.24 and an analyst price target of US$100.80, so the stock sits below both the internal cash flow estimate and the external target while earnings have grown at 8.4% per year over five years and 11.7% over the past year.
  • Consensus narrative supporters point to digital transformation, LIHTC driven fee income and Midwest loan growth as reasons this valuation gap may exist, and the figures highlight where the thesis lines up with current performance.
    • The 11.7% earnings growth over the last year, on top of a 5 year compound rate of 8.4%, fits with the idea that affordable housing demand, regional loan growth and expanding wealth management are already contributing to profit, even before any future step up from technology investments.
    • However, the fact that the P/E sits below the peer average of 14.4x while consensus still builds in margin compression from 36.2% to 27.8% shows that the market price may be giving weight to execution risks around digital projects, LIHTC exposure and the expected US$3 million Durbin impact as the bank approaches US$10b in assets.
🐂 QCR Holdings Bull Case

data.title: Rajratan Global Wire Limited Just Missed Earnings - But Analysts Have Updated Their Models
data.description:
Rajratan Global Wire Limited (NSE:RAJRATAN) last week reported its latest full-year results, which makes it a good time for investors to dive in and see if the business is performing in line with expectations. It was not a great result overall. Although revenues beat expectations, hitting ₹12b, statutory earnings missed analyst forecasts by 10%, coming in at just ₹13.81 per share. Earnings are an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance, look at what the analysts are forecasting for next year, and see if there's been a change in sentiment towards the company. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from Rajratan Global Wire's three analysts is for revenues of ₹13.2b in 2027. This reflects a decent 13% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to soar 28% to ₹17.63. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of ₹13.2b and earnings per share (EPS) of ₹21.43 in 2027. So there's definitely been a decline in sentiment after the latest results, noting the real cut to new EPS forecasts.

View our latest analysis for Rajratan Global Wire

The consensus price target held steady at ₹542, with the analysts seemingly voting that their lower forecast earnings are not expected to lead to a lower stock price in the foreseeable future. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. The most optimistic Rajratan Global Wire analyst has a price target of ₹597 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at ₹480. Even so, with a relatively close grouping of estimates, it looks like the analysts are quite confident in their valuations, suggesting Rajratan Global Wire is an easy business to forecast or the the analysts are all using similar assumptions.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. The analysts are definitely expecting Rajratan Global Wire's growth to accelerate, with the forecast 13% annualised growth to the end of 2027 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 7.5% per annum over the past five years. Other similar companies in the industry (with analyst coverage) are also forecast to grow their revenue at 13% per year. Factoring in the forecast acceleration in revenue, it's pretty clear that Rajratan Global Wire is expected to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. They also reconfirmed their revenue estimates, with the company predicted to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at ₹542, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

data.title: We Think That There Are Some Issues For Waaree Renewable Technologies (NSE:WAAREERTL) Beyond Its Promising Earnings
data.description:
Following the solid earnings report from Waaree Renewable Technologies Limited (NSE:WAAREERTL), the market responded by bidding up the stock price. Despite this, our analysis suggests that there are some factors weakening the foundations of those good profit numbers.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

A Closer Look At Waaree Renewable Technologies' Earnings

In high finance, the key ratio used to measure how well a company converts reported profits into free cash flow (FCF) is the accrual ratio (from cashflow). The accrual ratio subtracts the FCF from the profit for a given period, and divides the result by the average operating assets of the company over that time. You could think of the accrual ratio from cashflow as the 'non-FCF profit ratio'.

Therefore, it's actually considered a good thing when a company has a negative accrual ratio, but a bad thing if its accrual ratio is positive. That is not intended to imply we should worry about a positive accrual ratio, but it's worth noting where the accrual ratio is rather high. That's because some academic studies have suggested that high accruals ratios tend to lead to lower profit or less profit growth.

Over the twelve months to March 2026, Waaree Renewable Technologies recorded an accrual ratio of 0.64. That means it didn't generate anywhere near enough free cash flow to match its profit. Statistically speaking, that's a real negative for future earnings. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of ₹1.6b, which is significantly less than its profit of ₹4.79b. Waaree Renewable Technologies' free cash flow actually declined over the last year, but it may bounce back next year, since free cash flow is often more volatile than accounting profits.

Note: we always recommend investors check balance sheet strength. Click here to be taken to our balance sheet analysis of Waaree Renewable Technologies.

Our Take On Waaree Renewable Technologies' Profit Performance

As we have made quite clear, we're a bit worried that Waaree Renewable Technologies didn't back up the last year's profit with free cashflow. For this reason, we think that Waaree Renewable Technologies' statutory profits may be a bad guide to its underlying earnings power, and might give investors an overly positive impression of the company. But on the bright side, its earnings per share have grown at an extremely impressive rate over the last three years. Of course, we've only just scratched the surface when it comes to analysing its earnings; one could also consider margins, forecast growth, and return on investment, among other factors. In light of this, if you'd like to do more analysis on the company, it's vital to be informed of the risks involved. In terms of investment risks, we've identified 1 warning sign with Waaree Renewable Technologies, and understanding it should be part of your investment process.

Today we've zoomed in on a single data point to better understand the nature of Waaree Renewable Technologies' profit. But there are plenty of other ways to inform your opinion of a company. data.title: Why You Might Be Interested In Macbee Planet, Inc. (TSE:7095) For Its Upcoming Dividend
data.description:
Macbee Planet, Inc. (TSE:7095) stock is about to trade ex-dividend in three days. Typically, the ex-dividend date is two business days before the record date, which is the date on which a company determines the shareholders eligible to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is an important date to be aware of as any purchase of the stock made on or after this date might mean a late settlement that doesn't show on the record date. Meaning, you will need to purchase Macbee Planet's shares before the 28th of April to receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 31st of July.

The company's next dividend payment will be JP¥55.00 per share. Last year, in total, the company distributed JP¥55.00 to shareholders. Last year's total dividend payments show that Macbee Planet has a trailing yield of 4.1% on the current share price of JP¥1340.00. Dividends are an important source of income to many shareholders, but the health of the business is crucial to maintaining those dividends. So we need to investigate whether Macbee Planet can afford its dividend, and if the dividend could grow.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

If a company pays out more in dividends than it earned, then the dividend might become unsustainable - hardly an ideal situation. Macbee Planet paid out just 14% of its profit last year, which we think is conservatively low and leaves plenty of margin for unexpected circumstances. A useful secondary check can be to evaluate whether Macbee Planet generated enough free cash flow to afford its dividend. The good news is it paid out just 24% of its free cash flow in the last year.

It's positive to see that Macbee Planet's dividend is covered by both profits and cash flow, since this is generally a sign that the dividend is sustainable, and a lower payout ratio usually suggests a greater margin of safety before the dividend gets cut.

See our latest analysis for Macbee Planet

Click here to see the company's payout ratio, plus analyst estimates of its future dividends.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Businesses with strong growth prospects usually make the best dividend payers, because it's easier to grow dividends when earnings per share are improving. If earnings fall far enough, the company could be forced to cut its dividend. That's why it's comforting to see Macbee Planet's earnings have been skyrocketing, up 25% per annum for the past five years. Macbee Planet earnings per share have been sprinting ahead like the Road Runner at a track and field day; scarcely stopping even for a cheeky "beep-beep". We also like that it is reinvesting most of its profits in its business.'

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. Since the start of our data, two years ago, Macbee Planet has lifted its dividend by approximately 40% a year on average. Both per-share earnings and dividends have both been growing rapidly in recent times, which is great to see.

Final Takeaway

Has Macbee Planet got what it takes to maintain its dividend payments? We love that Macbee Planet is growing earnings per share while simultaneously paying out a low percentage of both its earnings and cash flow. These characteristics suggest the company is reinvesting in growing its business, while the conservative payout ratio also implies a reduced risk of the dividend being cut in the future. Macbee Planet looks solid on this analysis overall, and we'd definitely consider investigating it more closely.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Lloyds Banking Group (LSE:LLOY) After £1.75b Buyback Plan?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Lloyds Banking Group at around £0.99 a share still offers value, the key question is how its current price stacks up against different views of fair value.
  • The stock price has moved by a 3.9% decline over the last 7 days, a 6.1% gain over the last 30 days, is down 0.4% year to date, and shows returns of 41.6% over 1 year, 134.8% over 3 years and 177.1% over 5 years. This naturally raises questions about how much of the story is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent news flow around Lloyds Banking Group has focused on ongoing discussions of the UK banking sector, regulatory attention on consumer outcomes, and the interest rate backdrop that influences margins and loan demand. These themes help explain why sentiment can swing, as they affect how investors think about banks' earnings resilience and capital strength.
  • Simply Wall St currently gives Lloyds Banking Group a valuation score of 2 out of 6. This sets up a closer look at how different methods like DCFs and multiples compare, and points to an even more complete way to think about value that will be covered at the end of this article.

Lloyds Banking Group scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Lloyds Banking Group Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a bank generates above the return that equity investors are assumed to require, then capitalises those surplus returns into an estimate of fair value per share.

For Lloyds Banking Group, the model starts with a Book Value of £0.71 per share and a Stable Book Value estimate of £0.83 per share, based on weighted future book value estimates from 7 analysts. On this equity base, analysts expect Stable EPS of £0.12 per share, sourced from weighted future return on equity estimates from 13 analysts.

The Cost of Equity is put at £0.07 per share, which implies an Excess Return of £0.05 per share. That excess is supported by an Average Return on Equity of 14.62%, which is above the assumed required return. This is then used to project how much value Lloyds could keep creating on its equity base over time.

Using this excess returns stream, the model arrives at an intrinsic value of about £1.85 per share. Against a current share price around £0.99, this implies the stock is 46.6% undervalued on this approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Lloyds Banking Group Price vs Earnings

P/E is a common way to value profitable companies because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that each share generates. In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk can support a higher “normal” or “fair” P/E, while slower growth and higher risk tend to point to a lower multiple.

Lloyds Banking Group currently trades on a P/E of 13.78x. This sits above both the Banks industry average of 11.18x and the peer group average of 11.02x, which might suggest a richer valuation if you only look at those simple comparisons.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio aims to refine this by estimating what P/E you might expect for Lloyds given factors like its earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks. For Lloyds, this Fair Ratio is 10.87x, which is lower than the current 13.78x. On this preferred multiple approach, the shares screen as somewhat expensive relative to that Fair Ratio.

For investors, the key question is whether these products deepen Seagate’s role in workflows that already rely on its data center drives, or mainly serve as incremental accessories in a segment where Western Digital, Samsung, and others also compete.

Investors may want to track how quickly the One Touch, FireCuda X Vault, and LaCie 8big Pro5 lines gain traction with retailers, system integrators, and creator communities, and whether Seagate starts breaking out more detail on consumer and prosumer storage within its segment reporting. data.title: Be Sure To Check Out Token Corporation (TSE:1766) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend
data.description:
It looks like Token Corporation (TSE:1766) is about to go ex-dividend in the next four days. The ex-dividend date is usually set to be two business days before the record date, which is the cut-off date on which you must be present on the company's books as a shareholder in order to receive the dividend. The ex-dividend date is important as the process of settlement involves at least two full business days. So if you miss that date, you would not show up on the company's books on the record date. This means that investors who purchase Token's shares on or after the 28th of April will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 30th of July.

The company's next dividend payment will be JP¥360.00 per share. Last year, in total, the company distributed JP¥360 to shareholders. Calculating the last year's worth of payments shows that Token has a trailing yield of 2.7% on the current share price of JP¥13440.00. Dividends are an important source of income to many shareholders, but the health of the business is crucial to maintaining those dividends. That's why we should always check whether the dividend payments appear sustainable, and if the company is growing.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

If a company pays out more in dividends than it earned, then the dividend might become unsustainable - hardly an ideal situation. That's why it's good to see Token paying out a modest 27% of its earnings. Yet cash flows are even more important than profits for assessing a dividend, so we need to see if the company generated enough cash to pay its distribution. What's good is that dividends were well covered by free cash flow, with the company paying out 22% of its cash flow last year.

It's positive to see that Token's dividend is covered by both profits and cash flow, since this is generally a sign that the dividend is sustainable, and a lower payout ratio usually suggests a greater margin of safety before the dividend gets cut.

View our latest analysis for Token

Click here to see how much of its profit Token paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Stocks in companies that generate sustainable earnings growth often make the best dividend prospects, as it is easier to lift the dividend when earnings are rising. Investors love dividends, so if earnings fall and the dividend is reduced, expect a stock to be sold off heavily at the same time. For this reason, we're glad to see Token's earnings per share have risen 14% per annum over the last five years. Earnings per share are growing rapidly and the company is keeping more than half of its earnings within the business; an attractive combination which could suggest the company is focused on reinvesting to grow earnings further. Fast-growing businesses that are reinvesting heavily are enticing from a dividend perspective, especially since they can often increase the payout ratio later.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. Token has delivered 14% dividend growth per year on average over the past 10 years. It's great to see earnings per share growing rapidly over several years, and dividends per share growing right along with it.

The Bottom Line

Is Token an attractive dividend stock, or better left on the shelf? We love that Token is growing earnings per share while simultaneously paying out a low percentage of both its earnings and cash flow. These characteristics suggest the company is reinvesting in growing its business, while the conservative payout ratio also implies a reduced risk of the dividend being cut in the future. Token looks solid on this analysis overall, and we'd definitely consider investigating it more closely.

data.title: Analysts Are Updating Their OBIC Co.,Ltd. (TSE:4684) Estimates After Its Full-Year Results

OBICLtd reported JP¥135b in revenue, roughly in line with analyst forecasts, although statutory earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥172 beat expectations, being 2.9% higher than what the analysts expected. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. We thought readers would find it interesting to see the analysts latest (statutory) post-earnings forecasts for next year.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from OBICLtd's twelve analysts is for revenues of JP¥148.0b in 2027. This reflects a solid 9.4% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to rise 5.8% to JP¥184. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥147.6b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥182 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

It will come as no surprise then, to learn that the consensus price target is largely unchanged at JP¥4,904. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. There are some variant perceptions on OBICLtd, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at JP¥5,800 and the most bearish at JP¥4,100 per share. There are definitely some different views on the stock, but the range of estimates is not wide enough as to imply that the situation is unforecastable, in our view.

These estimates are interesting, but it can be useful to paint some more broad strokes when seeing how forecasts compare, both to the OBICLtd's past performance and to peers in the same industry. We can infer from the latest estimates that forecasts expect a continuation of OBICLtd'shistorical trends, as the 9.4% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2027 is roughly in line with the 9.8% annual growth over the past five years. Compare this with the broader industry, which analyst estimates (in aggregate) suggest will see revenues grow 5.0% annually. So it's pretty clear that OBICLtd is forecast to grow substantially faster than its industry.

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that there's been no major change in sentiment, with the analysts reconfirming that the business is performing in line with their previous earnings per share estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at JP¥4,904, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.
